quoted_tweet.text:
RIP Starving Artists.

Pls Donate isn’t the only one being affected by this update as well. The developers (and contributors me included) weren’t notified before on this either. This doesn’t only affect revenue wise, this affects opportunity wise on Roblox on things. It gave me a choice and meet amazing people including in RDC.

This really shows how no matter what Roblox game you’ve made or how much it impacted, or if it won an award. The company itself can kill it with one update so suddenly without notice in the future. Not only this affects developers, it affects the trust on how it proceeds itself as a BRAND COMPANY.

kept.text:
As a parent, I can see the "safety" thing behind this. Having lived an experience where I saw my young daughter get hacked, and someone used pls donate to steal all of her Robux, I recognize the safety implementation.

HOWEVER... making a decision that affects so many without any consideration of their wellbeing or their thoughts is NOT how you run a company. Developers are losing trust in Roblox by the day, and continuing to ostracize them will only drive them to leave the community altogether—NO devs... no Roblox.

Locking things behind a paywall is also very bad form. I HIGHLY recommend you look into this further and find a better solution that could still benefit those less fortunate and unable to pay for a Roblox Plus subscription. Also... why is this safety stuff suddenly a concern now when the Roblox Plus subscription model dropped? Didn't you pick a donation game (starving artists) to win a builderman award just recently?

https://t.co/899Vz71ZKC

profile_bio.description:
Environment Artist & Digital Fashion Designer (100M+ sales). 
‘25 Best UGC Creator RIA Winner. 
Formerly @Roblox @EpicGames @NinjaTheory. Also known as Yourius.

quoted_tweet.text:
Roblox is removing cross-game gamepass sales and forcing a $5/month requirement just to donate, with a 1,000 Robux monthly limit. This will kill PLS DONATE.

PLS DONATE is built around cross-game gamepass sales, a system that’s free for players and has no limits. Removing it doesn’t just change things, it kills the game completely.

I wasn’t made aware of this change before it was announced. No warning, no chance to give my input or feedback before they announce this change.

I’ve been developing on this platform since I was 12 and have always loved making games on Roblox, so seeing something this impactful happen overnight without real consideration is genuinely upsetting. This means a lot to me and I’m really sad about this.

The solution Roblox gave us is the Transfer API, which really isn’t a solution and still affects us heavily. It’s locked behind a $5/month subscription, capped at 1,000 Robux per month, and under-18 users require parental approval for EVERY SINGLE transaction. That completely changes the experience.

If transferring Robux is going to be paid, gamepasses need to coexist. Removing them ruins everything and doesn’t stop bad actors from just purchasing through the site anyway. It doesn’t make sense.

I really don’t want to build hatred toward this platform. I love Roblox and still believe in it, and if it truly cares about its developers, I’m sure there are better solutions that keep users safe without killing our game.

@Roblox @DavidBaszucki

We should opposed every 4th amendment destroying bill like the GUARD act.

Soon this will be coming for you for every device you use to access the net.

Time to fight.

quoted_tweet.text:
Roblox will no longer allow games to sell gamepasses from other games starting from May 29th.

As a mandatory replacement, Roblox is introducing the Transfers API. You must be age-checked or have a linked parental account to either receive or send Robux. Any Robux will immediately be received upon you accepting the Robux. You will receive 90% of the Robux, instead of the previous 60%; however, 10% still goes to the game's creator. To send Robux, you must be subscribed to Roblox Plus. Roblox currently only allows a 1,000 Robux maximum to be sent per month.

You will not be able to cancel any transfer. Additionally, you can transfer Robux on the "Buy Robux" page or by donating it to a user's page.

If you are under the age of 18, your parent must approve each transfer request. Any request not accepted within a week will be sent back to the person who initially sent it.

Any Robux earned from transfers are not DevEx-able.

quoted_tweet.text:
Roblox will no longer allow games to sell gamepasses from other games starting from May 29th.

As a mandatory replacement, Roblox is introducing the Transfers API. You must be age-checked or have a linked parental account to either receive or send Robux. Any Robux will immediately be received upon you accepting the Robux. You will receive 90% of the Robux, instead of the previous 60%; however, 10% still goes to the game's creator. To send Robux, you must be subscribed to Roblox Plus. Roblox currently only allows a 1,000 Robux maximum to be sent per month.

You will not be able to cancel any transfer. Additionally, you can transfer Robux on the "Buy Robux" page or by donating it to a user's page.

If you are under the age of 18, your parent must approve each transfer request. Any request not accepted within a week will be sent back to the person who initially sent it.

Any Robux earned from transfers are not DevEx-able.

quoted_tweet.text: @Roblox @FIMUSFIM Please make sure to also fix the stupid alt account enforcement bans. They are inherently flawed considering no one can even play Roblox on any public network without fear of being falsely banned. I’m talking schools, hotels, shopping malls… Honestly the best way is to just scrap it. They do much more harm than good, and it doesn’t even stop actual malicious people from using the platform as they can easily bypass it. Please Roblox, think about all your good players, and make the right decision.

profile_bio.description: Creator of @MedoTemplates

quoted_tweet.text:
Roblox is removing cross-game gamepass sales and forcing a $5/month requirement just to donate, with a 1,000 Robux monthly limit. This will kill PLS DONATE.

PLS DONATE is built around cross-game gamepass sales, a system that’s free for players and has no limits. Removing it doesn’t just change things, it kills the game completely.

I wasn’t made aware of this change before it was announced. No warning, no chance to give my input or feedback before they announce this change.

I’ve been developing on this platform since I was 12 and have always loved making games on Roblox, so seeing something this impactful happen overnight without real consideration is genuinely upsetting. This means a lot to me and I’m really sad about this.

The solution Roblox gave us is the Transfer API, which really isn’t a solution and still affects us heavily. It’s locked behind a $5/month subscription, capped at 1,000 Robux per month, and under-18 users require parental approval for EVERY SINGLE transaction. That completely changes the experience.

If transferring Robux is going to be paid, gamepasses need to coexist. Removing them ruins everything and doesn’t stop bad actors from just purchasing through the site anyway. It doesn’t make sense.

I really don’t want to build hatred toward this platform. I love Roblox and still believe in it, and if it truly cares about its developers, I’m sure there are better solutions that keep users safe without killing our game.

@Roblox @DavidBaszucki
